title: scleromitrion scabrum
intro: Scleromitrion scabrum, also known as rough-leaved Scleromitrion, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Scleromitrion scabrum are used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to provide various health benefits, including relief from fever, cough, and sore throat. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its anti-inflammatory properties are thought to aid in wound healing and skin conditions like eczema.
